fields.name: Journal Template Name
fields.tooltip:
Shows the name of the Insurance journal template.
The program automatically retrieves the name from the Name field in the Insurance Journal Template table.

fields.name: Journal Batch Name
fields.tooltip:
Shows the name of the journal batch.
The program automatically retrieves the name from the Name field in the Insurance Journal Batch table.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Shows the journal line's number.
The program fills in the field automatically and uses the number to keep track of the various lines in the journal.

fields.name: Insurance No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the insurance number to post an insurance coverage entry to.

fields.name: FA No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related fixed asset.

fields.name: FA Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the fixed asset.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date to use as the posting date on the insurance coverage ledger entry.

fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the appropriate document type for the amount you want to post.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date from the document that is the reason for the journal line. Typically this will be the date of a purchase invoice.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number for the journal line.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a document number that refers to a customer's or vendor's numbering system. You can enter a maximum of ten characters, both numbers and letters.
You can then use the number later if, for some reason, you need to search for the posted journal line using the customer's order number.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total amount the journal line consists of. Credit amounts must be entered with a minus sign.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description that is entered in the Insurance No. field.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reason code, a supplementary source code that enables you to trace the entry.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip:
Shows the source code that has been entered on the journal line.
The program automatically retrieves the source code from the Source Code field in the Insurance Journal Template table.

fields.name: Index Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to post an indexation (that is, to index the total value insured).

fields.name: Posting No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the number series that will be used to assign a document number to the journal line when it is posted. To see the number series that have been set up in the No. Series table, click the field.
The program automatically copied the number series code from the Posting No. Series field on the journal batch. You can change the code.
If this field is blank when you post the line, the document number in the Document No. field will be used on the posted line.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.
